mission.title: Install and Configure In Toto on Kubernetes
mission.description: in-toto is a framework designed to protect the integrity of the software supply chain by verifying that each task is performed by authorized personnel and that the product remains untampered during transit. Installing in-toto allows you to create a secure and verifiable software supply chain layout.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami Helm repository to your Helm configuration to access the in-toto chart.
```shell
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the in-toto Helm chart
steps.description:
Install the in-toto chart using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.
```shell
helm install in-toto bitnami/chainloop --namespace in-toto --create-namespace --version 4.0.75
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the in-toto pods are running successfully.
```shell
kubectl get pods --namespace in-toto
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the in-toto deployment to ensure efficient resource usage.
```shell
kubectl patch deployment in-toto --namespace in-toto --type='json' -p='[{"op": "add", "path": "/spec/template/spec/containers/0/resources", "value": {"limits": {"cpu": "500m", "memory": "512Mi"}, "requests": {"cpu": "250m", "memory": "256Mi"}}}]'
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the in-toto release from your cluster.
```shell
helm uninstall in-toto --namespace in-toto
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were installed with in-toto.
```shell
kubectl delete crd chainloops.chainloop.bitnami.com
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Remove the namespace used for in-toto to clean up any remaining resources.
```shell
kubectl delete namespace in-toto
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current release configuration.
```shell
helm get values in-toto --namespace in-toto > in-toto-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date to access the latest chart versions.
```shell
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the in-toto release
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the in-toto release to the latest version available.
```shell
helm upgrade in-toto bitnami/chainloop --namespace in-toto --version 4.0.75
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the in-toto pods are running and the upgrade was successful.
```shell
kubectl get pods --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the in-toto pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources or node issues.
```shell
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the pod logs for errors during startup.
```shell
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace in-toto
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, verify that the service is correctly configured and check the endpoints.
```shell
kubectl get svc --namespace in-toto
kubectl get endpoints <service-name> --namespace in-toto
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of in-toto will result in the in-toto pods running without errors, and you will be able to verify the integrity of your software supply chain. You can further configure resource limits and monitor the deployment as needed.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Helm and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.
